programme.title: Splash and Bubbles: I Only Have Eyespots for You/ Double Bubbles

programme.title: Sesame Street: Astronaut Elmo

programme.title: Pinkalicious & Peterrific: Peter's Blues/ Pink Raspberry

programme.title: The Great British Baking Show: Biscuits

programme.title: Doc Martin: All My Trials

programme.title: Doc Martin: All My Trials

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries: Mortal Coil

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Descent

programme.title: Modern Family: The One That Got Away

programme.title: Modern Family: Dude Ranch
programme.desc: The family vacations at a dude ranch in Wyoming where Jay squares off against a surprising foe. Mitch and Cam want to share news about their pending adoption plans, but suddenly realize that Lily may not be as ready as they are.

programme.title: Modern Family: When Good Kids Go Bad
programme.desc: Lily strongly disagrees with Mitchell and Cameron when they announce their plan to adopt another child. Manny is suspected of stealing a locket from school, and Jay tries to get him to confess. Claire goes to the extremes to prove she is right.

programme.title: Modern Family: Phil on Wire
programme.desc: The growing bond between Jay and his dog is grating on Gloria's nerves. Phil and Luke embark on a new adventure involving a tightrope, while Claire missteps as she tries to teach the girls a life lesson.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: After the shocking destruction of WWE Icon Edge by Randy Orton last week, what's next for Orton, Edge, and the RAW WWE Superstars?

programme.title: The Biggest Loser: Time for Change

programme.title: Modern Family: Aunt Mommy

programme.title: Modern Family: Virgin Territory
programme.desc: It's a day of disturbing realizations when Mitchell manages to ruin one of Jay's proudest golf moments, Phil overhears something that may forever change how he sees his little girl and Gloria stumbles upon Claire's dangerous little secret.

programme.title: Modern Family: Flip Flop
programme.desc: Phil is pretty confident in his ability to sell Claire and Cam's flipped house, but when it proves to be a little harder than he thought, he recruits the whole family into taking some desperate, borderline crazy measures.

programme.title: Dateline: Spy Games
programme.desc: The mysterious and near fatal shooting of an American security analyst outside Washington, DC may be linked to the murder of a Russian agent in London.

programme.title: The Biggest Loser: Time for Change
programme.desc: Twelve people who want to get healthy and transform their lives arrive at The Biggest Loser Campus.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Night
programme.desc: Detectives investigate the rape and murder of a woman who is found near a nightclub.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Blood
programme.desc: When a girl is assaulted, her attackers steal her car and toss her baby.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Parts

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Ripped

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Strain

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Name

programme.desc: After years in prison, Edward Honaker finally found someone who believed him, and was willing to pay for DNA testing which could prove he was innocent.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Speck of Evidence
programme.desc: On September 17, 1984, in a suburb of Tucson, Arizona, eight-year-old Vicki Hoskins left home on her pink bicycle to mail a letter for her mother. She never returned.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Without a Prayer
programme.desc: Madalyn Murray O'Hair and her two top lieutenants disappeared with no shortage of suspects. Her abduction may never have been solved if it weren't for the discovery of a headless, handless body, and the serial number on an artificial hip.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Clutch of Witnesses
programme.desc: Two eyewitnesses see a truck intentionally drive over a sleeping man outside a motorcycle gang's clubhouse. During the investigation the witnesses, the truck, and the body disappear, leaving police just one clue: a bloodstain in the snow.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Shoe-In for Murder
programme.desc: A 12 year-old is the only witness to her mother's death and is unable to provide many details. Over a decade later, forensic evidence has lead the investigation right back to where it started.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Waste Mis-Management
programme.desc: A middle-aged woman vanishes from her apartment complex. When the police find few leads at the scene of the crime, they realize a nearby dumpster may hold the key to all the answers.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Material Evidence
programme.desc: Examine intriguing crimes, accidents and outbreaks of disease from around the world.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Kill'igraphy
programme.desc: A man considered unstable by his neighbors is accused of killing his wife, but the medical examiner disagrees.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Hand Delivered
programme.desc: An anonymous 'tip' referring to unpublicized aspects of an unsolved case, leads police on a hunt for a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Death Play
programme.desc: Marie Robard's character in her high school's play 'Hamlet' hinted at her own inner turmoil.

programme.desc: Police investigate when a severed leg is found in the garbage. When they realize they can't use conventional techniques to catch the killer, police must use scientific techniques like DNA testing and toxicology reports to find him.

programme.desc: The story of nurse Tanya Reid who may have murdered her own baby and was planning to murder her son.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Biggest Street Race Ever
programme.desc: JJ Da Boss has invited 8-street racing teams from around the country to Memphis for a chance to win $100,000. A win won't come without a fight, though, as South Carolina's small-tire racers and St. Louis high-dollar drivers are ready for war.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: David meet Goliath
programme.desc: The heat is on as the South Carolina underdogs bring the fight to the St. Louis crew. It's all on display as mouthy leadership, fired-up tempers, and high pressure situations dominate a tension-filled night.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Full Throttle: We Got Bad Blood
programme.desc: With added facts and bonus content: Kye Kelley and his NOLA racers take on Birdman and his team from Texas, and things get heated. The rivals' history of bad blood boils over when NOLA has car trouble, and Texas refuses to play nice.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Full Throttle: First-Round Knockout
programme.desc: With added facts and bonus content: The war continues between Team NOLA and Texas as they battle to see who can race best under pressure. The team who can stay focused on the street stands the best chance of winning.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: North vs. South
programme.desc: Chris Rankin and his ragtag team from the Northeast land in Memphis to take on Quinn's family of racers from Kentucky. Big-money cars and even bigger egos collide on the streets to create the perfect storm of high-speed strategy and chaos.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Round and Around
programme.desc: Farmtruck and AZN invite all the 405 racers out to Willow Springs International Raceway for a road race competition. The racers have to pair up to compete in a time attack competition where the pair with the fastest combined times will take home $10,000.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: North vs. South
programme.desc: Chris Rankin and his ragtag team from the Northeast land in Memphis to take on Quinn's family of racers from Kentucky. Big-money cars and even bigger egos collide on the streets to create the perfect storm of high-speed strategy and chaos.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Shine On You Crazy Diamond
programme.desc: Ryan Martin looks over his car while Chief and Dave push each other in a competitive testing session, as they all get ready for another chance at number one. At race time, the racers fight to move up the list until broken cars slow everyone down.

programme.title: I Was Prey: Grisly Encounters
programme.desc: Jordan Carbery is investigating a noise outside his cabin and encounters a massive grizzly bear. Days before their destination-wedding celebration, Zanele Fox and her husband take a river tour when she is thrown from her raft and ravaged by a crocodile.

programme.title: I Was Prey: One Wrong Step
programme.desc: While vacationing in South Africa, Peter Knottenbelt suddenly finds himself in the jaws of a massive crocodile. In Nova Scotia, Meg Doyle is walking her beloved dog when they're suddenly ambushed by a pair of angry raccoons.

programme.title: I Was Prey: All Alone
programme.desc: During an archery hunt in Cranbrook, British Columbia, Chad Dueck comes face-to-face with a mother grizzly bear. In Tracy, California, Walt Dean King is savagely attacked by a Brahma Bull roaming his neighborhood.

programme.title: I Was Prey: Night Terror
programme.desc: On family trip to the Canadian wilderness, 12-year-old Zac Delventhal is dragged from his sleeping bag by a hungry wolf. While swimming in the Pacific Ocean, Maria Korcsmaros is attacked by a Great White shark.

programme.title: I Was Prey: Terror in Paradise
programme.desc: On a family trip to Pattaya, Thailand, Helen Costigan is attending an elephant show when one of the massive beasts turns deadly. Golf ball recovery specialist Scott Lahodik is diving in a Florida lake when he's attacked by a vicious alligator.

programme.title: I Was Prey: Deadly Decision
programme.desc: Sandy Prince is swimming in an African river when she's attacked by a massive crocodile. While mountain-biking in Washington State, Phil Anderson comes face to face with a hungry cougar.

programme.title: I Was Prey: Mauled in the Mountains
programme.desc: Louie van Grootel is working in the Canadian wilderness when he's attacked by a massive grizzly bear. In Colorado, Travis Kauffman is trail-running and comes face to face with a hungry mountain lion.

programme.title: I (Almost) Got Away With It: Got to Escape Through a Toilet
programme.desc: Lifelong pals and criminals Timothy Fletcher and Doni Brown break out of a Florida prison in 2009. Anxious for freedom, the two need money. A robbery that ends in murder puts them on most wanted lists and leads cops on a manhunt across the Southeast.

programme.title: I (Almost) Got Away With It: GOT a Handsome Face
programme.desc: Steven Ray Milam is addicted to robbing banks. After a string of heists, he's dubbed the Handsome Guy Bandit, thanks to a clever disguise. But his pride and a costly mistake put the FBI on his trail. He's forced to leave his family and go on the run.

programme.title: I (Almost) Got Away With It: GOT to Get Home to Mother
programme.desc: After getting handed a stiff sentence for robbery, Robert Nielsen flees the brutality of prison by escaping and living under the radar with his family in California. But even they can't shield the young fugitive from the challenges of being on the run.

programme.title: I (Almost) Got Away With It: GOT to Crawl My Way to Freedom
programme.desc: After a botched robbery, Darryl Norris faces murder charges and a long prison sentence. He pulls off a brilliant escape through the ceiling of his cell and plans to flee the country. But authorities are determined to find him and lock him up for good.

programme.title: I (Almost) Got Away With It: GOT to Be Part of the Pittsburgh Six
programme.desc: Andrew Heim is doing time for assault at Western Penitentiary in Pennsylvania. When a prison buddy finds a way to tunnel out of the prison Heim and five other inmates escape. The group is dubbed 'The Pittsburgh Six' and sparks a nationwide manhunt.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Daddy Dearest
programme.desc: Emily and her very opinionated father want the perfect princess ball gown for her big day. Bride Danielle's father passed away, and she hopes to find a gown he would have loved.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Designer Dreams
programme.desc: Liz is in the salon with Danielle Jonas, and wants to show off her new body. Courtney Cason said yes to Mark Zunino's sketch, but now it's time to try on the real gown. Jillian wants a fashion forward dress but her mom isn't making it easy.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Coco Is Not Impressed

programme.desc: A cell phone and a pawnshop slip provide valuable clues to a murder; a woman's body is found on the railroad tracks.

programme.title: The First 48: Blood Red/ Deadly Moves
programme.desc: In Dallas, two men on their way to visit family meet a hail of bullets. In Cleveland, a preacher is found strangled in his home.

programme.title: Alaska PD: Arctic Survival

programme.title: Alaska PD: Love and Hate in Alaska
programme.desc: In Kotzebue, Sgt. Norman Hughes and Officer Robert Odom grapple with the distrust of the police as they try to keep the community safe. In Fairbanks, two officers bring a personal touch in dealing with alcohol and drug-fueled crime.

programme.title: Alaska PD: Wild Wild West
programme.desc: In Kotzebue, Sgt. Norman Hughes gets more than he bargained for on a domestic violence call while Ofc. Robert Odom faces the dangers of working alone.

programme.title: Alaska PD: Taste of Blood
programme.desc: In Fairbanks, a wanted fugitive flees into the woods--and the police's only lead is frozen footprints in the snow. When a killer bear becomes loose in Kodiak, officers must hunt it down--before it kills again.

programme.title: The First 48: Under One Roof/ Off Target
programme.desc: A grandfather is robbed and murdered in his bedroom for a 26-inch TV. Also, a drive-by shooting in Overtown left an innocent young woman dead.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Simon Soft
programme.desc: Michael turns iced tea into a tea candle and later, at a pet store, he reveals that caterpillars have wicks. Plus, at a warehouse, Michael stumps his assistant when he proves that big tricks come in shrink-wrapped packages.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: The Food Flattener
programme.desc: At a cooking class, Michael gets crabby when chopping salad and brings the heat to instantly cook a meatball with his breath. Later, Michael chills his assistant to her bones when a cryotherapy experiment freezes.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Up Loser's Creek
programme.desc: Find out which joker ran for Congress as a write-in candidate, which joker was a top-notch salesman before starting the show and who had a job in finance.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Rubbed the Wrong Way
programme.desc: Find out why the food court was picked for the 'stalk and text' challenge, what classic comedy inspired 'over the shoulder,' and who was lucky enough to test out the flying contraption for Joe the Genie.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Baggage Shame
programme.desc: Find out how Murr's old neighbour learns a little TMI about his personal life and which of the jokers Joe would want by his side in a fight.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Flatfoot the Pirate
programme.desc: Who made the call to give Murr a face full of slushie at the mall? How did the Jokers keep their props secret from each other before Laugh Man Standing? And whose breast milk did Pirate Sal chug?

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Quantum Mock-Anics
programme.desc: Find out which joker gets dizzy when a cab has a strong air freshener, who can speak Spanish, and which joker struggles to keep a straight face during Joe's 'expert' punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Remember the Pact
programme.desc: Find out how many mousetraps accidentally went off during Q's turn in 'hold this please,' what made Joe and Murr join 'team skins' in Columbus park, and how Murr's tech punishment made show history.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Spider Man
programme.desc: Find out which of the jokers is the handiest, who hates the taste of beer, and how Q's love of animals beat out his arachnophobia and made for a better punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Silence of the Lame
programme.desc: Find out who keeps the tags on his jacket so he can return them, which crew member voices the 'sexy beast' commercial, and what chain restaurant the guys used during Sal's 'shush!' punishment at the mall.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Walking Dread
programme.desc: Find out which joker has a black thumb, what challenges the guys like so much they play when the cameras are off, and how long it took to zombie-fry the undead in Sal's sewer punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Lost Boy
programme.desc: Find out which joker once worked as a fish monger, who lied on his resume to snag a TV internship and what chain restaurant the guys used during Sal's 'shush!' punishment at the mall.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Washed Up
programme.desc: How the phrase “rooty toot toot” became popular on set, which guy has recently cut his potato intake in half, and how Joe managed to cool off during his sweltering bikini car wash punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Party Crasher
programme.desc: Find out which jokers were in on the surprise Gary Busey appearance, where the phrase 'tit for tat' came from, and how Murr's big mouth backfired on him, leading to this Statue of Liberty punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Make Womb for Daddy
programme.desc: Find out who scored free sandwiches while shooting at Blimpie; what classic game inspired the 'Guess What' challenge; and what was worse for Q than the pain he endured during his 'childbirth' punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Sneaking Number Twos, Going Number One
programme.desc: Find out why the jokers love filming at the grocery store, how many pencils were purchased for put-pocketing and why Sal's pee punishment was twice as terrible as it looked.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Turning the Tables
programme.desc: Find out how the Jokers keep their cover while filming at the mall, and Q's favorite VR word. Plus, learn how long Joe was sore after the table-busting punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: No Child Left Behind
programme.desc: Find out how you make a 'Grandpa Whoopsie' doll, why winter is the perfect season to play pin for the win and where the idea for Q's dirty diaper challenge came from.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Puppification
programme.desc: Michael blends juices with a sticker at an appliance store, and then reverses the food chain ay a pet shop. Later, at a doggy spa, a rejuvenation machine turns back time.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Vanishing Daycare
programme.desc: Michael transforms a t-shirt at a thrift store, and his aid at a daycare thinks she loses more than her mind. Plus, Michael's helper at a museum runs scared when a sculpture slithers away.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Moving Dragon
programme.desc: While working at a pool store, Michael flies on a kickboard. Then, at a pet store, he breeds hamsters from pellets. He also stuns his helper on moving day with a fiery statue.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Lipstick and the Flying Nun
programme.desc: While working at an antique shop, Michael unpacks an item with a message from beyond. Plus, he works a fundraiser where a painting raises not only money but the body of a nun.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Dummy Talks
programme.desc: At an appliance store, a man goes bananas over Michael's self-stocking fridge. Then, Michael speeds up the life cycle of a frog in a science lab, and he pulls the strings at an estate sale when a haunted doll toys with his assistant.

programme.title: The Carbonaro Effect: Fright at the Museum
programme.desc: At a campsite, Michael turns rain into wine and has an app that gives new meaning to the 'call of nature.' Later, Michael gets thorny at a plant shop, and in a museum, he drums up ancient history that grasps for the present.

programme.desc: Trisha is making a boxed lunch for Garth. She makes a turkey meatloaf sandwich, Gwen's coleslaw, Mari's oatmeal cookies and pineapple sweet tea.

programme.desc: Trisha Yearwood's good friend, Mandy, has finally made her home in Nashville. Mandy wants to throw a party in her new backyard and invite all her friends and neighbors. Recipes include chicken wings and chorizo quesadillas.

programme.title: Trisha's Southern Kitchen: Big Game Day
programme.desc: Trisha Yearwood and her friend, Glenda, are looking back at Trisha's best game day recipes that have scored points with party guests over the years. Nashville hot chicken meatball sliders and Philly cheesesteak pull-apart bread are sure to please.

programme.desc: Trisha Yearwood is a huge fan of the Nashville Predators, so she's invited them over to eat, hang out and laugh about the time Ryan Johansen dragged Glenda across the ice. The game-day spread includes loaded curly fry nachos, Philly cheesesteak and more.

programme.desc: When Platt goes to her father Robert's for dinner, she warns him to be careful with his new girlfriend, Natalie. As Platt is leaving her father's place, she is assaulted and rushed to Chicago Med.

programme.desc: When a car plows through a crowd of concert-goers and gunfire erupts, Lindsay and Ruzek are on the scene. Unsure of the motive and concerned about terrorism, they order Homeland Security to assist in the investigation.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: A War Zone
programme.desc: Intelligence is tasked with tracking down the dealers of fentanyl, a drug that has been linked to multiple overdoses around the city. As they investigate possible sources, the team uncovers a series of dealers in a college smuggling ring.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Some Friend

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Don't Bury This Case

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Don't Read the News

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: You Wish

programme.title: The Simpsons: The Canine Mutiny

programme.title: The Simpsons: The Old Man and the Lisa

programme.desc: When an island off the coast of Mexico suddenly disappears, government officials scramble to find an explanation; cutting-edge technology might reveal if the strange place was destroyed by a foreign power or if it existed at all.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Chernobyl's Nuclear Monsters
programme.desc: Strange circles of reindeer appear in the remote tundra of Sweden, experts investigate if this mysterious event could be evidence of fatal aftershocks from the Chernobyl nuclear disaster.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Nazi Doomsday Forest
programme.desc: When mysterious structures are discovered deep in a remote forest, experts investigate if this strange site was the long-lost birthplace of a top-secret Nazi project that produced some of the deadliest weapons the world had ever seen.

programme.title: What on Earth?: Bermuda Triangle of Space

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Star Apocalypse
programme.desc: New discoveries help experts investigate the strange phenomenon of stars suddenly dying throughout the cosmos, and using cutting-edge tech, astronomers are revealing the universe's most terrifying secret.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Mystery of Planet 9
programme.desc: Scientists have discovered a mysterious ninth planet on the very edge of our solar system. Ten times the size of Earth, this strange world could have moons with extraterrestrial life.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Supernovas
programme.desc: The universe is expanding and so is our understanding of it. In this brand new episode, up to date information on the big bang is given. The universe grew from a point, smaller than an atomic particle, to the vast cosmos we see today.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Stars That Kill
programme.desc: The universe is home to real-life Death Stars that could destroy planet Earth. The latest science reveals the terrifying secrets of NASA's six deadliest: vampire stars, supernovas that can vaporize a planet, and unpredictable stars gone rogue.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Mystery of Planet 9
programme.desc: Scientists have discovered a mysterious ninth planet on the very edge of our solar system. Ten times the size of Earth, this strange world could have moons with extraterrestrial life.

programme.title: How the Universe Works: Death of the Last Stars
programme.desc: Our universe's stars are dying off faster than new ones are born, and using the latest technology, experts investigate the secrets of the last stars of the cosmos and what this stellar apocalypse means for life on earth.

programme.title: The Planets and Beyond: Alien Planets: Cosmic Nightmares
programme.desc: Astronaut Mike Massismino uncovers the violent secrets of the most deadly planets outside our solar system.

programme.title: The Planets and Beyond: Mercury: The Cursed Planet
programme.desc: Astronaut Mike Massimino explores Mercury, a deadly and tiny planet that faces constant attacks from the Sun, and which might one day pose a threat to all life on Earth.

programme.title: The Planets: Saturn: Mysteries Among the Rings
programme.desc: Astronaut Mike Massimino reveals the mysterious secrets of Saturn and its rings. Using the latest science from the Cassini mission, he explores the planet's giant icy geysers, powerful hurricanes, and the moon that may be hiding extraterrestrial life.

programme.title: The Planets: Venus: The Hell Planet
programme.desc: Astronaut Mike Massimino explores Venus, a hellish planet covered in active volcanoes and dense clouds. Using cutting-edge technology, he decodes the secrets beneath this volatile planet and investigates if Earth could be heading towards the same fate.

programme.title: Monster Black Holes: Hawking's Giants
programme.desc: Stephen Hawking changed everything we know about black holes, but even today, scientists don't know how the very biggest were formed. Now, new discoveries might finally solve the mystery of supermassive black holes and reveal the secrets of our galaxy.

programme.title: The Unexplained Files: Omens of the Mothman

programme.title: The Unexplained Files: Peruvian Alien Skull and Baltic Sea UFO

programme.desc: In 1895, a lighthouse is built to warn ships of perilous rocks in the Flannan Isles. Historian Keith McCloskey investigates the theories after three lighthouse keepers disappear. A Russian police captain investigates the strangest case of his career.

programme.desc: A couple of opposites come together to form a power couple, but, they soon learn that their money can't buy love and happiness, it can only buy death.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Love Sick
programme.desc: Daphne Abdela, Lisa Michelle Lambert and Sarah Kolb appear to be baby-faced schoolgirls, but don't be deceived. When they thought someone was after their boyfriends, they became cold-blooded killers.

programme.title: Deadly Women: A Family Tragedy
programme.desc: Home is where the heart is and a killer lurks.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Kiss Then Kill
programme.desc: Deadly women "kiss then kill" when love goes wrong.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Lover's Revenge
programme.desc: When these women were betrayed, they demanded payback. A crazed mother took a custody battle too far, a scorned lover wiped out her competition, and a sex addict stepped on everyone in her way.

programme.title: Deadly Women: To Have and to Kill
programme.desc: When some brides vowed to love and honor their husbands, they lied. These Deadly Women made a mockery of marital commitment. They only promised 'To Have and To Kill.'

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Alaskan Temptress
programme.desc: A Washington community is shocked when PTA mom, Mechele Linehan is arrested for murder from a decade earlier in Alaska. Details of a seedy and sordid past emerge. But is this suburban mother a cold-blooded temptress or has she been wrongly accused?

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: The Long Island Serial Killer: The Lost Girls
programme.desc: When a vivacious young escort, Shannan Gilbert, vanishes on Long Island's south shore in 2010, her disappearance leads police to discover the bodies of ten other victims dumped by a serial killer.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: In the Name of Love
programme.desc: Pvt. Tracie McBride has just arrived for military intelligence training when she's kidnapped from a laundry room at Goodfellow Air Force Base in Texas. A hunt for the missing soldier leads to a tale of horror involving a decorated war vet.

programme.title: American Monster: Right Before Your Eyes
programme.desc: When a good-looking, successful businessman meets an aspiring actress, she appears to be the perfect match. But the honeymoon doesn't last long. The relationship takes a turn...a deadly turn.

programme.title: American Monster: Wave to Daddy
programme.desc: The daughter of one of America's most notorious killers gives an exclusive interview, taking us on a journey into the eyes of a cold-blooded monster. Never-before-seen footage reveals the devastating double life of a murderer and his horrific crimes.

programme.title: American Monster: The Last Broadcast
programme.desc: After a taping of their local-access TV show in rural Vermont, Chrissy and Charlie Billis have a terrible accident, smashing their car into a tree. Only one survives. But tapes from the couple's TV show suggest a sinister tale behind what really happened.

programme.title: American Monster: Just Out of Frame
programme.desc: A fun-loving couple, both business-owners, share a close relationship with their grown daughters. So when the mother is found gruesomely bludgeoned to death in her own home, no one suspects the unthinkable. But unlike guilty people, video doesn't lie.

programme.title: Nazi Fugitives: Franz Stangl and Gustav Wagner
programme.desc: See how Nazi hunter Simon Wiesenthal tracked down fugitive war criminal Franz Stangl in Brazil. Stangl was responsible for the murder of over 800,000 people.

programme.title: Nazi Fugitives: Paul Touvier
programme.desc: French investigators hunt down Paul Touvier, a pro-Nazi sympathizer who was responsible for murdering both Jews and resistance fighters. This 'Hangman of Lyon' hid himself with the assistance of a right wing group associated with the Catholic Church.

programme.title: World War II in Color: Victory in Europe
programme.desc: As 1945 began, Hitler's Third Reich was entering its death throes. In the west, Allied forces were within striking distance of the Rhine. In the east, a Red Army blitzkrieg brought Soviet forces to the River Oder just an hour's drive from Berlin.

programme.title: World War II in Color: The Island War
programme.desc: By the spring of 1943, the Pacific war had reached a turning point. The United States had stopped the Japanese advance, now it launched a two-pronged attack to drive it back. To achieve this, the U.S. Navy had to pioneer new forms of warfare.

programme.title: World War II in Color: The Gathering Storm
programme.desc: On September 1st 1939, the most destructive war the world has ever known began as German troops invaded Poland. By the time it ended six years later, more than 70 million people would be killed and whole continents devastated.

programme.title: World War II in Color: Britain at Bay
programme.desc: By the end of June 1940, Hitler was victorious in Western Europe. Nazi Germany now controlled Denmark, Norway, the Netherlands, Belgium and France. Only Britain remained defiant. But the Fuehrer was certain that it could not hold out for long.

programme.title: World War II in Color: Closing the Ring
programme.desc: By late summer 1944, the Allied forces in north-west Europe were thrusting towards the German border. Paris and Antwerp were liberated, but then supply problems brought the advance to a halt. Meanwhile, Stalin had halted his main thrust towards Berlin.

programme.title: World War II in Color: Overlord
programme.desc: By early 1944, Hitler faced a new threat. In the West, Allied preparations to invade northwest France were coming to a climax. It was an attack he had long been expecting. His problem was knowing when and where it would come.

programme.title: World War II in Color: Britain at Bay
programme.desc: By the end of June 1940, Hitler was victorious in Western Europe. Nazi Germany now controlled Denmark, Norway, the Netherlands, Belgium and France. Only Britain remained defiant. But the Fuehrer was certain that it could not hold out for long.

programme.title: World War II in Color: Closing the Ring
programme.desc: By late summer 1944, the Allied forces in north-west Europe were thrusting towards the German border. Paris and Antwerp were liberated, but then supply problems brought the advance to a halt. Meanwhile, Stalin had halted his main thrust towards Berlin.

programme.title: World War II in Color: Overlord
programme.desc: By early 1944, Hitler faced a new threat. In the West, Allied preparations to invade northwest France were coming to a climax. It was an attack he had long been expecting. His problem was knowing when and where it would come.

programme.title: World War II in Color: The Gathering Storm
programme.desc: On September 1st 1939, the most destructive war the world has ever known began as German troops invaded Poland. By the time it ended six years later, more than 70 million people would be killed and whole continents devastated.

programme.title: World War II in Color: The Island War
programme.desc: By the spring of 1943, the Pacific war had reached a turning point. The United States had stopped the Japanese advance, now it launched a two-pronged attack to drive it back. To achieve this, the U.S. Navy had to pioneer new forms of warfare.

programme.title: World War II in Color: Victory in Europe
programme.desc: As 1945 began, Hitler's Third Reich was entering its death throes. In the west, Allied forces were within striking distance of the Rhine. In the east, a Red Army blitzkrieg brought Soviet forces to the River Oder just an hour's drive from Berlin.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Bull Run
programme.desc: In what will be the first real battle of the Civil War, untested and poorly trained Rebel and Confederate troops clash on the blood soaked banks of the Bull Run River.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Antietam
programme.desc: Following a string of victories in Virginia, Confederate military genius General Robert E. Lee brings his army north in a bold attempt to end the Civil War and win Southern Independence.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Fredericksburg
programme.desc: Following the strategic Union win at Antietam, federal forces clash with a massive Rebel army in the town of Fredericksburg, Virginia in what will become the largest fight of the Civil War.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Gettysburg
programme.desc: An elite band of Yankee Sharpshooters must stop Robert E. Lee's Alabamian shock troops from flanking the Union line and winning the battle of Gettysburg and perhaps the entire Civil War.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Nashville
programme.desc: In a daring attempt to regain the upper hand in the Civil War, Confederate forces try to retake the city of Nashville and sever the supply line that's feeding the Union invasion of the south.

programme.title: The Civil War: Brothers Divided: Battle of Petersburg
programme.desc: The Union invasion of the south has ground to a halt at the seemingly impregnable ring of trenches built around Petersburg, Virginia. After eight bloody attempts to storm them have failed, two officers from Vermont will lead a daring raid.

programme.title: American Lawmen: Allan Pinkerton: Saving Lincoln
programme.desc: Celebrated P.I. Allan Pinkerton is hired to protect a strategic railroad from attack, but as his investigation unfolds, he uncovers a hidden conspiracy to assassinate President Lincoln.

programme.title: American Lawmen: Melvin Purvis: The Gangbuster
programme.desc: During the Great Depression, a violent crime wave is sweeping the nation. It was up to the FBI's Melvin Purvis to lead a massive manhunt for the most dangerous men in America.

programme.title: American Lawmen: Petrosino vs. the Mafia
programme.desc: Turn-of-the-century Little Italy is a dangerous place, but as New York City's first Italian-American detective, Joe Petrosino is determined to do whatever it takes to protect his people.

programme.title: Against the Odds: Those Damned Engineers
programme.desc: In December of 1944, the seemingly defeated Germans secretly mustered 600,000 battle-hardened troops, including whole SS Panzer Divisions, into an all out offensive to capture Antwerp, the heart of the Allied supply chain.

programme.title: Restored: 1906 Arts and Crafts House
programme.desc: Brett is called upon to restore a historically significant 1906 arts and crafts home. He brings it back to its original glory by reintroducing the closed-in fireplaces, re-instating the floor plan and replacing the 1980s kitchen.

programme.title: Restored: 1920s Spanish Eclectic
programme.desc: A couple find a cute 1920s Spanish eclectic home in which they want to update the kitchen, add an ensuite bathroom and dressing room to the master, and plant a drought-tolerant garden, all staying in Spanish style.

programme.title: Restored: Mystery of the English Cottage
programme.desc: A young family has purchased a 1920s English cottage in Redlands, California, for their forever home. Something is funky with the living room and they can't figure out what. Brett solves the mystery, removing all the 1970s additions.

programme.title: Restored: 1910 Arts and Crafts Family Farmhouse
programme.desc: Brett restores a 1910 arts and crafts farmhouse for a family who relocates from the big city to the countryside of Cherry Valley, California. They hope to become organic farmers and want to include elements for the family farm and business.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: The Honeymoon Suite
programme.desc: In a Maine Cabin Masters first, Chase and his creative crew help a newlywed couple take a questionable 'wedding gift' and turn it into an alpine ski bungalow that would rival those found in the Swiss Alps.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Teaching an Old Cabin New Tricks
programme.desc: For the past 20 years, a family has created lasting memories of their cabin overlooking Harpswell Sound, but the cabin is in need of some work to see the next 20 years and beyond.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Pilsbury Family Rafting Cabin
programme.desc: Builder Chase Morrill teams up with his brother, sister and best friend to save and transform abandoned Maine cabins. They give these forlorn properties the facelift they've needed for decades. You never know what you might find when you go for a walk.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Desert Pond Hideaway
programme.desc: Chase and the team travel to a secluded piece of land close to Desert Pond to help a couple turn their forgotten shack into a relaxing retreat. The couple have big ambitions leaving the crew stretching a $30,000 budget in only four weeks.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Teaching an Old Cabin New Tricks
programme.desc: For the past 20 years, a family has created lasting memories of their cabin overlooking Harpswell Sound, but the cabin is in need of some work to see the next 20 years and beyond.

programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Love Yurts: Namaste Yurt
programme.desc: Nathan and Jenny Toler meet up with clients Sara and Shawn who want to expand their current home in Haleiwa, Hawaii, to include a multi-functional 16-foot yurt that will serve as a yoga studio and lounge space.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: Summer Kitchen Restoration
programme.desc: Lauren and Dan own an amazing stone home in Montgomery County dating back to the 1850s which has a separate summer kitchen. The exterior is in great condition but the interior is crumbling.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: 1803 Second Floor Overhaul
programme.desc: Carpenter Jeff Devlin heads to the home of Jodie and JP to check out the original second floor of their 1803 stone home. The space has been sectioned off over time, and the homeowners want a more cohesive master suite that evokes the home's history.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: 1751 Two-Floor Restoration
programme.desc: A mother and daughter bring Jeff in to restore two rooms in the original portion of their 1751 stone home. Jeff and his team give the dining room a slightly formal farmhouse feel. Then they achieve a rustic and refined style in the bathroom.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: 1732 Kitchen Relocation
programme.desc: Jeff visits a couple at their 1732 home to address their wish to build a new kitchen in their living room. During the renovation, they face a few bumps as they knock down a wall, relocate the plumbing and try to bring some history back to the space.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: 1790 Three-Room Restoration
programme.desc: Jeff meets Allen and Kathleen, a couple who wants him to restore three rooms in the original portion of their 1790 home. Jeff and his team dig into each space, uncovering a few unique original details and adding other historic features.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: 1737 Entryway and Kitchen
programme.desc: Lisa and Tom ask Jeff to work on their 1737 stone home in Plymouth Meeting, Pennsylvania. They ask him to address the first floor and the kitchen. Jeff and his team bring unique 1800s elements into the entryway room and give the kitchen an 1800s overhaul.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: 1735 Master Bedroom Retreat
programme.desc: Jeff meets up with a homeowner to restore the living room and bedroom in the oldest portion of her 1735 stone home. Jeff lowers an historic mantel in the living room and turns the master into a retreat with a wall of stone.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: 1800s Kitchen and Dining Room
programme.desc: A couple have just bought an historic stone home with portions built in the 1700s, 1800s, and 1990s. They want Jeff to look at their dining room, kitchen and sunroom. They want a more open floor plan, but Jeff will have to move stone walls.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: 1800s Living Room and Kitchen
programme.desc: Fred and Elizabeth call on carpenter Jeff Devlin to restore two spaces in their 1800s stone home. Their living room lacks historic character, and their kitchen sits in a 1950s addition, with dated metal cabinets, pine paneling, and linoleum floors.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: 1690s Living Room Restoration
programme.desc: Jeff is recruited by a couple to do a true restoration of the original living room in their 1690s stone home. They also want to enclose the attached vestibule to create a mudroom. They want it to blend in with the rest of the home with stone walls.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: 1802 Farmhouse Overhaul
programme.desc: Jeff is challenged to bring out the history in an 1802 farmhouse that has had a number of questionable renovations throughout its 200 years. His clients want original stone and ceiling beams in the dining room and an overhaul of the fireplace.

programme.title: Stone House Revival: 1750s Inn Revival
programme.desc: A couple that's in over their heads with their home that was once a historic inn calls on Jeff to turn two rooms into a tavern-like entertaining space. The team quickly find issues when they uncover the fireplace and try to install an exposed beam.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Meek Farmhouse
programme.desc: Mike and Robert remove a two-piece staircase from a farmhouse in Marion, VA. The guys also pick through a train yard. Back at the shop, Mike builds a bar from panel doors as a surprise for a customer's husband.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Hylton Victorian
programme.desc: The Black Dog Salvage team saves parts of an old Victorian-style farmhouse in Laurel Forks, VA.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Pinehurst Steam Plant
programme.desc: The Dawgs head to Pinehurst, NC, to salvage an old steam plant that used to run the town. They remove some cool valves and find extra salvage in a shed near the building.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Portside Marina
programme.desc: The Dawgs save the bow and stern of a boat on Smith Mountain Lake in Moneta, VA. The team cuts up an old Chevy van for a commissioned wall art piece. New dogs Molly May and Stella make their debut.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Mine Master's House
programme.desc: The Salvage Dawgs travel to Quakertown, Pennsylvania, in order to reclaim items from an old mine master's house. They are surprised to find much of the house picked over before they arrive.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Rural Retreat Historical Society
programme.desc: Mike, Robert and the team travel to the Rural Retreat Historical Society in Rural Retreat, Virginia. Mike sets up rigging from the rooftop for an outdoor clock while Robert salvages a vault door and bank teller station.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Nacho, Nacho Man
programme.desc: Noah Cappe tries some of the wackiest and wildest nacho combinations on the midway.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Bacon 2: The Sequel
programme.desc: Bacon is the star of its own blockbuster sequel and host Noah Cappe is at the front of line to savor even more bacon-tastic fairground favs.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: The Devil Wears Pasta
programme.desc: Noah Cappe gets things started in Florida at the Okeechobee County Fair where he samples the Hot Wing Grilled Cheese, the Sweet Bacon Devil Burger and the made-in-Florida Strawberry Margarita Funnel Cake.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Bite Club
programme.desc: Noah Cappe celebrates the arrival of spring in Winchester, Va., at the Shenandoah Apple Blossom Festival. He starts the day with the Cowboy Breakfast and ends it with Caramel Apple Tots.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Philadelphia, Pa
programme.desc: Casey Webb sails to Philadelphia to tackle the epic Classic Whale Challenge, a 13-pound bagel sandwich stuffed with cream cheese, nova salmon, whitefish, tomatoes and onions.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Ozark Mountains
programme.desc: Casey travels over the Ozarks to tackle the Goliath, a massive cupcake equal to 22 regular-sized cupcakes.

programme.title: Man v. Food: San Francisco
programme.desc: Adam devours a Mission Style Super Burrito, ginormous sandwiches, and a two-gallon monster sundae.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Cincinnati, OH
programme.desc: Casey Webb heads to Cincinnati to see if he can take down an enormous eight-pound, double-crusted pizza in just thirty minutes. Along the way, he encounters one beautiful cheeseburger at a 200-year-old bar and a massive tray of Cincy-style chili.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Asian Sensations
programme.desc: Take your taste buds on a trip to the Far East with a list of America's best Asian dishes. Martha Stewart shares a perfect meaty powerhouse in New York City; Alex Guarnaschelli uncovers a heavy-hitting pork bun in Minneapolis, and more.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Trigger Foods
programme.desc: We're losing all control and giving in to ultimate food cravings. Geoffrey Zakarian starts things off like never before with the most juicy and succulent roast chicken from New York City. Jonathon Sawyer takes it to the next level.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Fried and Gone to Heaven
programme.desc: Some fried food is so crispy, so crunchy and so incredible that you feel like you've died and gone to heaven. Jeff Mauro heads to Proxi in Chicago for a food frenzy with the most-unique version of street corn ever imagined. Simon Majumdar takes his taste.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Stacked
programme.desc: With layers upon layers stacked with flavour and ingredients, these favorite dishes seem too good to be true. Wolfgang Puck gushes about an unimaginable seafood platter at Cassia in Los Angeles.

programme.title: Good Eats: Let Them Eat (Icebox) Cake
programme.desc: Alton resuscitates the languishing tradition of the 'icebox' or 'refrigerator' cake. These no-bake cakes were all the rage in the 1950s, but they’re ready for a pastry redux.

programme.title: Good Eats: Devil of a Cake
programme.desc: Alton Brown reinvents the classic Devil's food cake and its cousin red velvet.

programme.title: Good Eats: Churn Baby Churn 2
programme.desc: After a visit from federal ice cream agents and investigating the ingredients, Alton cracks the code of making homemade ice cream.

programme.title: Good Eats: American Classics V: A Pound of Cake
programme.desc: Alton considers the pound cake to be an American classic.

programme.title: Beach Bites With Katie Lee: Beach Bar Snacks
programme.desc: Beach life brings up visions of tasty food, great cocktails and relaxing fun times, and Katie Lee is getting a taste of the amazing bar bites brewing along the beaches of the world.

programme.title: Beach Bites With Katie Lee: Doing the Island Hop

programme.title: Beach Bites With Katie Lee: Family Recipes
programme.desc: Katie Lee is hitting the beaches for tried and true family dishes. From Puerto Rican-tinged Soul Food to a Cuban-influenced ice cream shop, Katie learns the origins of some delicious dishes as well as the heart, soul and story behind them.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls: And the Attack of the Killer Apartment

programme.title: Martin: I've Got a Secret
programme.desc: Pam, Tommy and Cole are angry at Martin and Gina when Martin reveals Pam's secret on his show. Martin and Gina's only friends left are Martin's boss Stan and Stan's girlfriend.

programme.title: Martin: Do the Fight Thing
programme.desc: Martin's radio fans love it when Gina and Martin have arguments on the show and Stan wants to permanently hire Gina as Martin's co- host.

programme.title: Martin: I Saw Gina Kissing Santa Claus
programme.desc: Martin reluctantly agrees to dress up as Santa Claus for Tommy's 'Big Brother' program.

programme.title: Martin: Break Up (Part One)
programme.desc: Martin and Gina break up after an argument over a Valentine's Day gift.

programme.title: Martin: Break Up (Part Two)
programme.desc: After the breakup with Gina, Cole and Tommy take Martin out to meet new women.

programme.title: Martin: Break Up (Part Three)
programme.desc: Realizing he made a mistake, Martin tries to win Gina back.

programme.title: Martin: Credit Card Blues
programme.desc: Martin and a friend find themselves in big trouble after committing credit card fraud.

programme.title: Martin: Jerome's in the House

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: When You Hit Upon a Star
programme.desc: Will's love affair with a musician is threatened when she meets her handsome ex-lover.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: Stop Will! In the Name of Love
programme.desc: Ashley rebels against Will's overprotectiveness when he chaperones her on a date.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: You'd Better Shop Around
programme.desc: Will withdraws from his university classes to become a successful car salesman.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: The Ol' Ball and Chain
programme.desc: On the day of Jazz's wedding, his bride-to-be makes sexual advances towards Will.

programme.title: Fresh Prince of Bel Air: The Harder They Fall
programme.desc: Will is stranded in a mountain forest with his new girlfriend's disagreeable father.

programme.title: Movie: How High
programme.desc: Two buddies end up in possession of a magical weed plant that enhances their brainpower.

programme.title: Movie: How High 2
programme.desc: Two young "potrepreneurs" embark on a magical weed-fueled journey across the city of Atlanta in search of their missing stash and some extra cash.

programme.title: Movie: Soul Plane
programme.desc: Last-minute passenger additions shake things up on an airline's maiden flight.

programme.title: Movie: From Friend to Fiancé

programme.title: Movie: Date With Love
programme.desc: After a-list actress, Alex Allen, has an epic Hollywood meltdown, she is in need of some positive publicity. Salvation comes in the form of a small town boy's video 'promposal' in which the student invites Alex to prom.

programme.title: Movie: Love at First Glance
programme.desc: A young woman's hope for a wedding is upended when her fiancé breaks up with her. On her train ride home, she finds herself trading glances with a handsome stranger who, as he exits the train, leaves his phone behind.

programme.title: Movie: A Valentine's Match
programme.desc: A reality TV host is fired from her job and so she returns home before Valentine's Day. While home, she finds herself running the town's festival, with her ex-fiancé and their two, scheming mothers.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Mister Terrific

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Mother's Day
programme.desc: While waiting for phone calls from their children wishing them a happy Mother's Day, the girls reminisce about past Mother's Days.

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Yes, We Have No Havanas

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The Days and Nights of Sophia Petrillo

programme.title: The Golden Girls: The One That Got Away

programme.title: The Golden Girls: Yokel Hero

programme.title: Frasier: The Kid

programme.title: Frasier: The 1000th Show

programme.title: Frasier: Voyage of the Damned

programme.title: Military Makeover: Operation Career
programme.desc: Homes and lives of veterans across the country are transformed by a team of experts.

programme.title: The Closer: Living Proof
programme.desc: A blood feud within a refugee family from Kosovo results in another victim, forcing Brenda and her team, along with Captain Raydor, to continue their investigation on Christmas Day.

programme.title: The Closer: An Ugly Game
programme.desc: The routine arrest of a crack addict exposes a questionable rehab facility and leads Brenda and her squad on a race to find a missing young woman. The case leads Fritz to reveal to Brenda some ugly truths about her past.

programme.title: The Closer: Unknown Trouble
programme.desc: A murder investigation leads the team to the owner of a music label; Brenda is sued.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: As We Know It
programme.desc: Despite the best efforts of Seattle Grace's staff, the code black situation escalates.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: Yesterday
programme.desc: Someone from Derek and Addison's past arrives in Seattle and shakes things up. Meanwhile, Burke learns about Christina's secret. After a visit with her mother, Meredith looks into more of her family's dirty little secrets.

programme.title: Grey's Anatomy: What Have I Done to Deserve This?
programme.desc: Meredith and George suddenly find themselves at odds at work. Bailey, although technically on maternity leave, finds herself treating Addison for an unusual feminine issue. Alex finds himself oddly jealous when Izzie pays more attention to Denny.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Silent Mite
programme.desc: Doug is convinced that a 'little person' stole his wallet while he was out shopping.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Awed Couple
programme.desc: When Deacon and Kelly find new friends, Doug and Carrie must turn to their old friends.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Cologne Ranger
programme.desc: Doug senses his manhood slipping away when Carrie forbids him to wear his new cologne.

programme.title: The King of Queens: Domestic Disturbance
programme.desc: Doug is unhappy with Carrie's choice for a housekeeper so he hires Spence's mom, Veronica.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Scout's Honor
programme.desc: Police determine that the human remains found in a discarded box belong to one Edna Posey. But to find out whether the man accused of the crime is guilty, investigators turn to insects and the forensic entomologist who can interpret their behavior.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Partners in Crime
programme.desc: It takes a long time and a very hot fire to cremate a human body, and thus destroy any evidence of foul play. But the medical examiner who performed the autopsy on the badly burned body of Charles 'Jack' Lynch found telltale clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Without a Trace
programme.desc: The deaths of a child and a truck driver in the same hospital on the same day are investigated to see if there is a link between them.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Grave Evidence
programme.desc: The shooting of a man during a hunting trip is reopened years after the incident, and the victim's hunting partner becomes a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Separation Anxiety
programme.desc: 'The Tracey Frame Case' - after a passer-by spots a wild fire in a secluded parking lot, police discover a dreadful homicide. It takes days to identify the victim, and most of the evidence is lost when firefighters extinguish the blaze.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Office Visit
programme.desc: "The Dr. Brian Stidham Case" A prominent Arizona surgeon is found stabbed to death outside his office. All signs point to a random attack until investigators uncover an unlikely suspect with deep pockets, and an even deeper hatred for the good doctor.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Burning Ambition
programme.desc: A Seattle policeman’s home is destroyed by an arsonist. One month later, the officer arrests a low-level drug dealer, who confesses to the arson. But when the suspect vanishes, investigators begin to question the authenticity of his confession.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Memories
programme.desc: A pregnant woman is brutally attacked in her own home and her unborn child is killed. After coming out of her coma, and regaining her memory, she identifies her husband as the perpetrator. A new DNA test lead investigators to a different suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Fire Dot Com
programme.desc: When a federal agency rules that a fire was intentionally set, the mother of the child killed in the suspicious fire was charged with murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Soft Touch
programme.desc: A case where a fingerprint left in a piece of cloth brought justice to a senseless murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Squire's Riches
programme.desc: A man perishes in a fire while working inside his garage. Initially, the coroner rules the death an accident. But a Forensic Odontologist changes the direction of the case, as police unravel a bizarre case of stolen identity.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Home of the Brave
programme.desc: When their trailer catches fire, the young husband heroically rescues his wife and infant son. A month later, his wife is beaten to death in a bedroom of his parents' home. The cut window screen points to an intruder, but there is a lack of evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Bitter Pill to Swallow
programme.desc: A pregnant woman experiences severe morning sickness and suspects something is amiss.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sip of Sins
programme.desc: Faryion Wardrip seemed like the model citizen to most, but he was also a brutal serial killer who, for years, eluded police without suspicion. But smart police work and DNA from a discarded paper cup proved to be Wardrip's undoing.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Invisible Intruder
programme.desc: A Texas woman claims that an intruder attacked her and killed two of her sons, but detectives question her story when they find some inconsistencies at the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Ultimate Betrayal
programme.desc: Fire sweeps through a mansion, killing two children, all evidence leads to the mother.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Bagging a Killer
programme.desc: Police use satellites to trace the kidnapper of a nine-year-old girl. After questioning whether the girl's long lost mother was somehow involved, the satellite tracking reveals who the kidnapper is, where they took her, and why.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Double Trouble
programme.desc: A Maine State Trooper is attacked during a routine stop which triggers a chain of events that jump starts a stalled murder case hundreds of miles away.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Killer's 'Cattle' Log
programme.desc: When police in the Great Plains are called to retrieve a dead body, they do a background check on the victim. The trail leads them into a bizarre web of homeless drifters, cattle auctions and bad checks.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Skin of Her Teeth
programme.desc: A human skull retrieved from a watery grave reveals a ghastly crime. Markings on the skull indicate deep knife wounds. The teeth had been removed from the skull, apparently so the killer could keep the victim's identity a secret.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Scout's Honor
programme.desc: Police determine that the human remains found in a discarded box belong to one Edna Posey. But to find out whether the man accused of the crime is guilty, investigators turn to insects and the forensic entomologist who can interpret their behavior.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Partners in Crime

programme.title: Forensic Files: Burning Ambition

programme.desc: Kim tries to keep traditions alive by planning a family ski trip to Montana, but there's no mistaking that this year's vacation has different vibe. On the slopes, Khloé risks life and limb to bond with Kylie.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: In the Blink of an Eye ...
programme.desc: In the mid-season finale, the siblings return home from Montana to wish their dad well as Bruce prepares to transition. Kris travels with Kendall to Paris, and Kim pursues one last hope of getting pregnant.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Mother Armenia
programme.desc: Kim and Khloé visit the land of their ancestors to fulfill a long-standing dream of their father's. Kourtney wants to figure out why Kylie is being so distant and the truth is something unexpected.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Keeping Up: Kardashians on Vacation
programme.desc: Travel around the world with Kim and the gang, as the defining moments from each of the Kardashian family vacations are recapped.

programme.title: Pop of the Morning
programme.desc: Jade Catta-Preta, new host of The Soup, joins us in the studio.

programme.title: The Daily Pop
programme.desc: Actress Jamie-Lynn Sigler and 'Bachelor' Peter Weber join us in studio. Plus, celebrity chef Wolfgang Puck gives us a preview of the menu at the Oscars Governors Ball.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Time to Dash
programme.desc: During a trip to Miami, a major department store makes an offer to buy Dash that leads to Kim, Kourtney, and Khloé arguing about the store's future. Later, Kourtney struggles to enjoy herself around Scott and Khloé keeps her new relationship from Kim.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: Paris
programme.desc: Kourtney is eager to experience Paris Fashion Week with Kim and see Kendall walk the runway for the first time, but their trip to the city of love takes a dark turn when Kim is robbed at gunpoint.

programme.title: Keeping Up With the Kardashians: The Aftermath
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Paris incident, Kim struggles to cope with frightening reminders of the robbery, Kendall wrestles over her decision to prosecute an unstable trespasser, and Kris makes it her mission to heighten security for the entire family.

programme.title: Chopped: Blue Plate Fate
programme.desc: Four modern diner chefs compete to transform the baskets into blue plate specials worthy of $10,000. In the appetizer round, the chefs must sling out dishes using some late night food and a classic three-ingredient sandwich.

programme.title: Chopped: Beat Bobby Flay, Part 1
programme.desc: Over three preliminary battles, Chef Bobby Flay sits on the judges' panel, as 12 returning 'Chopped' champions sweat and scramble to get to the grand finale. Bobby himself competes in a title fight against the champ of all champs.

programme.title: Chopped: Beat Bobby Flay, Part 2
programme.desc: Four new returning champs battle with all they've got to determine who will be a finalist. In the appetizer basket, a seafood that needs to be tenderized and a canned product that needs to be incorporated.

programme.title: Chopped: Beat Bobby Flay, Part 3
programme.desc: A set of twins is among the returning 'Chopped' winners striving for the opportunity to beat Bobby Flay. A cold soup and a stuffed lobster challenge the champs, and keeping track of all four ingredients proves problematic for some.

programme.title: Chopped: Beat Bobby Flay, Finale
programme.desc: Ferocious repeat champs return to the Chopped Kitchen to bring all they've got to the first two rounds to see who gets to face Bobby Flay!

programme.title: Chopped: $50,000 Champs Challenge: Part 3
programme.desc: The fiercest Chopped champions are back to defend their titles in a whirlwind, no-holds-barred battle. A frozen oddity throws them a curve ball in the first round, and they find a box of something sweet plus a yummy Chinese specialty in the entree basket.

programme.title: Chopped: Smokin' Skills
programme.desc: The chefs are excited to face smoked-food challenges requiring them to make delicious modern dishes with the ancient cooking method. The chefs left standing at the end must make do with a fishy candy in the dessert round.

programme.title: Chopped: Pasta Possibilities
programme.desc: Four chefs face a three-round culinary battle where pasta is found on every plate. They start off the carb-tastic competition by making pasta appetizers that include pea tendrils and a fatty, delicious fish.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Bon-Appé-Treehouse

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: City Sleeker Treehouse
programme.desc: Pete is back in Texas to build a sophisticated, sleek-and-chic treehouse in the heart of Dallas. Daryl and the NTS team put treehouse engineering to the ultimate test at Washington State University, proving down-to-the-pound the power of the TAB.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Treehouse Utopia
programme.desc: Pete partners up in Utopia, TX for his first-ever joint venture: a treehouse B&B built for romance.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Shaq Takes it to the Trees!
programme.desc: It's a slam dunk when Pete builds an all-star speakeasy for basketball legend Shaquille O'Neal. The ball is in Pete's court to design a decagonal structure with a soaring 25 ft high ceiling and extra wide stairs to accommodate Shaq and his size 22 shoes.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Branched Out: Luxury in the Limbs
programme.desc: Pete and his team are bringing modern luxury to the canopy in these builds as they head from Texas to Pittsburgh for two discerning clients.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Treetop Medieval Fortress
programme.desc: Pete builds a citadel fortress for a Texan couple who loves all things medieval. A 25 ft-high tower will hold a round table and thrones, as well as a glass floor loft. A metalsmith joins the build to craft medieval touches, like a dragon door handle.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Mindbending Silver Maple
programme.desc: Inspired by tales of a time long past, Pete builds a treehouse cradled by the stems of a twisty silver maple on the shore of Lake Chelan. The cedar-shingled structure has a curved turret that soars two storeys high and a deck to take in views of the lake.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Branched Out: Luxury in the Limbs

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Sky High Redwood Retreat
programme.desc: An arborist pushes the team's skills to the limit, requesting a tri-level residence a dizzying 60 feet up in three giant redwood trees. Pete and the crew take the comforts of home to new heights in this bohemian chic treehouse.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Antonio Brown's Steel City Skybox
programme.desc: Pete makes a treehouse touchdown in Pittsburgh for Antonio Brown, wide receiver for the Steelers. The game plan is to build a luxury skybox with a two-storey window wall that overlooks a custom basketball court and football field.

programme.title: The Crocodile Hunter: America's Deadliest Snakes
programme.desc: Steve and Terri travel across the U.S. in search of remarkable rattlesnake species. Steve has a close call with Timber Rattlers in Virginia. In Florida, they find snakes as diverse as the Pigmy Rattler and the large Eastern Diamondback.

programme.title: Too Cute!: Rainbow Colored Kittens
programme.desc: In a litter of six kittens, the only boy struggles to fit in with his sisters.

programme.title: My Cat From Hell: A Brave New Cat World

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet: A Fighting Spirit
programme.desc: Dr. Jeff helps a puppy after it is kicked in the head by a donkey, and later goes fishing for a hook stuck in a hungry beagle's throat. Dr. Amy helps a cat unable to walk and Dr. Nichols gets caught up in a wrestling match with an alpaca.

programme.title: The Vet Life: Lights, Camera, Action!
programme.desc: The clock is ticking as Dr. Blue performs an emergency C-section on a mother with two puppies stuck in her uterus, while Dr. Ross and Dr. Lavigne work on pets with serious eye issues. The vets test their on-camera skills to create a video for the clinic.

programme.title: Crikey! It's the Irwins: Robert Trains a Dragon
programme.desc: Robert and Indah, a Komodo dragon, participate in an exciting research experiment. Terri tries to save a koala joey with a zero percent chance of survival. Bindi friends a childhood favourite spot- Oregon Coast Aquarium.

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo: Capybara Casanova
programme.desc: Capybara Lily is pregnant and keepers are hoping for a big litter to liven things up in the all-female group. When newborn Henry emerges, he quickly learns to enjoy having the run of the paddock, and the attention of all the females.

programme.title: The Zoo: A Warthog Named Peaches
programme.desc: Peaches, a two-month-old warthog, charms his keepers while training to become an animal ambassador. A nervous one-horned rhino adjusts to her new exhibit. Plus, Willow, a red panda, shows signs of being pregnant.

programme.title: Pit Bulls & Parolees: Low Rider
programme.desc: A dog's chance at a forever home is put at risk when a tumor is found. Tia faces a tough decision when an aggressive dog becomes a danger to her staff. VRC tries to reunite a stray dog with its owner.

programme.desc: Andy and Ashley take on a rundown house in the transitioning Hurst neighborhood. They hope it will be a fast flip, but with rats running rampant and an old tree on the roof, they have their work cut out for them.

programme.title: Flip or Flop Fort Worth: Welcome to the Jungle
programme.desc: Rundown and beat-up is the best way to describe Andy and Ashley's next flip in West Fort Worth, but they're up for the job. Things are going great until the house rears all its problems, causing their reno bill to skyrocket.

programme.title: Desert Flippers: Go Bold or Go Home
programme.desc: Lindsey and Eric go big restoring a termite-infested midcentury gem. The duo makes some bold design choices such as vibrant orange tiles, a pink front door and a cocktail grove addition in the back yard.

programme.title: Desert Flippers: Bid to Win
programme.desc: When Eric and Lindsey Bennett roll the dice and bid on a property sight unseen, their winning bid looks like a good bet at first glance. The well-maintained home has an outdated style but no major red flags.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: Behind the Design: From Lackluster To Retro-Modern
programme.desc: Joanna designs a house for her sister, brother-in-law and their five children. Jo blends elements of vintage, modern and retro styles in a cohesive way for her sister.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: Behind the Design: The Ramsey House
programme.desc: Joanna blends the clean and simple look of Scandinavian design with the textures and patterns of Moroccan style in an 1890's farmhouse. She also finds a way to make the upstairs fit for five kids.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Raise the Roof
programme.desc: Tamara gets creative renovating an expansive 1950s ranch-style home. She updates the exterior with faux stone and adds another floor to the 3,000-square-foot home, but rain and an old fireplace force her to rethink her plans and budget.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Stairway to Master Suite Heaven
programme.desc: Tamara Day uses her 1,400-square-foot second-story addition to create three new bedrooms in a 1950 ranch home.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Bumming About the Plumbing
programme.desc: Tamara tackles a 1905 Kansas City Shirtwaist home that's short on bathrooms but not short on charm. She restores the front door, updates the fireplace and renovates the kitchen, but struggles with sewer and roof issues.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Mirror, Mirror on the Bathroom Wall
programme.desc: Tamara Day takes on a 1905 Kansas City Shirtwaist that has only one bathroom. She doubles the size of the walk-in closet and enlarges the master suite, but her hopes of updating the home's bedrooms start to sink when her contractor discovers a leak.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Tamara's Tudor Tutorial
programme.desc: Tamara Day takes on a 1927 Tudor by blowing out the existing small kitchen and outfitting it with a large island and eating nook. She transforms a bedroom into a dining room, but her hopes of refurbishing the deck begin to break apart.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Shower Showstopper
programme.desc: Tamara takes on a behemoth 1927 Tudor house, but while she admires the home's original details, her dad discovers a problem. She uses a kitchenette to create a bar, revamps an uneven patio and enlarges the master bathroom.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Catwalk Craziness
programme.desc: Tamara Day starts on a 1900 Kansas City Shirtwaist-style home containing a living room with a catwalk instead of a ceiling. She pulls up linoleum flooring and gives the damaged foyer staircase a facelift.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: When One Barn Door Closes
